characters.description: A Tier 3 Challenger forced into a new arena at maximum growth. Cut off from ordinary supplies and the density needed for his usual methods, he must adapt quickly to survive the trials and resume his urgent search for his cousin Niya.
characters.name: Jiran

characters.description: Jiran's noble mentor and a cursed Unique ascender. He is not present in the arena, but the ring that could summon him is destroyed during Jiran's entry, cutting off a possible route to aid while leaving Samris's situation unresolved.
characters.name: Feylon Samris

characters.description: Jiran's Tier 5 cousin, a healer-in-training and capable fire combatant. When shadow entities overrun her trainee settlement, she helps protect the escaping recruits and flees with the survivors through an underground route.
characters.name: Niya

characters.description: During the assault on Niya's training settlement, Dommel guides recruits toward safety. He recognizes the hostile shadows as Markhiss's power and escapes underground with Niya.
characters.name: Dommel

characters.description: An absent but suspected source of the shadow ability used in the lethal attack on Niya's settlement. His connection to the assault makes him an immediate threat, although his location, purpose, and precise involvement are not yet established.
characters.name: Markhiss

characters.description: A speaker for the musical, tree-like Timberlings in the Valley of Melodies. After Jiran heals her critically wounded birther and another injured member of her people, Luara resolves to proclaim him the great spirit they have awaited.
characters.name: Luara Luaterappa

characters.description: Jiran's partner and a representative of her displaced people. By the time Jiran returns to the People's Cavern, she has departed with Olive and Cameron to meet an Imperial representative, leaving him uncertain about the mission's outcome.
characters.name: Mayalyn

characters.description: A member of Mayalyn's traveling party, accompanying her and Cameron on a mission to meet an Imperial representative. She is away from the People's Cavern when Jiran returns, and the reason for the meeting has not yet been explained.
characters.name: Olive

characters.description: A companion traveling with Olive and Mayalyn on a mission to meet an Imperial representative. He is away from the People's Cavern when Jiran returns, and the purpose and outcome of the meeting remain unexplained.
characters.name: Cameron

characters.description: An entity linked to Jiran's revelation ability and to Madra. Though she does not appear beside him, her eye-shaped marker guides his interface map toward Niya, providing crucial direction while her nature and limits remain uncertain.
characters.name: Daughter

characters.description: A figure with whom Niya spent several seasons during her separation from Jiran. The conditions of that period prevented her from safely discussing family or friends, and she has not yet disclosed the full history of their connection.
characters.name: Silence

characters.description: A powerful Tier 7 duchess who investigates the plundered mountain temple. After finding evidence that its secret has been breached, she follows the intruder's ice-mana signature, needing a capture to protect her already damaged political standing.
characters.name: Ravenna

characters.description: The Tier 7 ruler of besieged Mortan. With the city's wards failing for reasons he cannot identify, he offers Mayalyn conditional protection and formal recognition for her people if Olive helps diagnose the defensive formations.
characters.name: Duke Reinhardt

characters.description: The cover identity Cameron gives Jiran inside Mortan because the nobility already knows Jiran's real name from an earlier search. The disguise lets him meet the city's leaders as the unknown fighter who destroyed the Graymin rooks.
characters.name: Mortimer of Malton

characters.description: Niya's absent friend in Lordstone and someone she urgently wants Jiran to help. Jiran's revelation confirms that Shara is alive and presently stable, but cannot determine how much time she has left, making her condition a serious unresolved obligation.
characters.name: Shara

characters.description: A high-ranking member of the Graymin collective sent south to pursue and assimilate the impossible new entity detected after Jiran begins his evolution. His pursuit remains an unresolved threat.
characters.name: Unnamed Graymin king

characters.description: A man whose strength is not explained learns that a baby has been born and sets out to examine the child. The destruction left across his former hunting area demonstrates his power.
characters.name: Unidentified powerful man

characters.description: The primary name of the Imperial princess previously known as Olive. By this point she has regained her lost arm through ascension and completed Jiran's aura-oneness training alongside his other companions.
characters.name: Oliviala LeCruex

characters.description: A sapient Remalon of immeasurable power that arrives during the battle with two Tier 7 Graymin. It immobilizes the battlefield and focuses on Jiran, taking control of a crisis whose purpose and intended outcome are still unknown.
characters.name: Unnamed Remalon tester

characters.description: An extraordinarily powerful visitor who intercepts Jiran and Mayalyn at a floating mountain. Although she appears elderly, Jiran recognizes her rank as an Empress before the encounter proceeds further.
characters.name: Unnamed Empress

characters.description: The informal title Mortan's soldiers give Jiran while publicly celebrating his part in saving the city. It joins his Mortimer cover identity as a name used by the defenders, reflecting his visible firepower and support for their brigades.
characters.name: Fireling

characters.description: The western Imperial ruler who questions Jiran about the Remalon's intervention at Mortan. Her elderly appearance conceals her true body and mana, and she permits him to continue through her territory while arranging observation.
characters.name: Western Empress

characters.description: Olive's father and Cameron's uncle, an Imperial ruler whose void aspect can erase nearby trees when his control slips. He confronts them over their dangerous actions and demands information about the Remalon incident and the man known as Mortimer.
characters.name: Emperor Dominus

characters.description: Jiran's mentor and Shara's grandfather. Shara identifies the family connection while Jiran examines the technique behind her illness, but Lenton remains offstage and unaware of the investigation after Jiran blocks immediate contact.
characters.name: Lenton

characters.description: A heavily armed Remalon leader with overwhelming mana. A sudden change in her people's status triggers a continent-wide alarm, and she connects the anomaly to an infant's return before ordering the implicated individual captured alive.
characters.name: Unnamed Remalon leader

characters.description: An Empress's daughter who regards Jiran as her prospective betrothed and is pursuing him. Olive tells her that he has already left the Empire, leaving the pursuit unresolved.
characters.name: Unnamed princess

characters.description: The dying sentient world connected to Jiran's champion status. Before this book begins, she placed stored mana within the damaged part of his soul, and her power and restrictions continue to shape what he can do.
characters.name: Madra
